scope: Player-facing light-signal indications commonly encountered during German shunting movements.

aspects.title: Stop for shunting movements
aspects.recognition: Two red lights horizontally arranged on a light shunting signal or integrated signal assembly.
aspects.meaning: Stop. Shunting movements must not pass the signal.
playerAction: Do not proceed until a valid shunting authority is displayed or given.

aspects.title: Shunting permitted
aspects.recognition: Two white lights rising diagonally from left to right.
aspects.meaning: The shunting movement may pass the signal.
playerAction: Remain prepared to stop short of vehicles, points or other obstructions.
Remember that Sh 1 is not authority for a train movement beyond the limits of the shunting movement.

aspects.title: Shunting permitted (former DR area)
aspects.recognition: Two white lights rising diagonally from left to right, visually matching the modern Sh 1 light display.
aspects.meaning: The shunting movement may pass the signal. The designation is associated with former Deutsche Reichsbahn practice.
playerAction: Treat the operational authority as a shunting movement, not a normal train movement.

notes: Sh 1 and Ra 12 can look the same to a player; the site should explain the regional and rule-system context rather than duplicate the visual without explanation.
Mechanical Sh 0 boards and other shunting signals remain for a later slice.
